title: Country & Americana Guitar
description: Country, Americana, and Nashville-style exercises demonstrating that the Zone–Tritone framework is genre-agnostic. Chicken-pickin' enclosures, pedal-steel bends, Nashville turnarounds, Travis picking, and country-blues hybrids all operate on the same harmonic physics as jazz and blues.
zt_rationale: Country guitar occupies a unique pedagogical position: it shares the dominant cycle and guide-tone mechanisms of jazz, the semitone-approach gravity of flamenco, and the pentatonic + blue-note tension of blues — all delivered in a clean-amp, open-string context that makes zone-crossing motion physically audible without harmonic density obscuring it.

chicken_pickin.label: Chicken-pickin' over I7
chicken_pickin.description: Hybrid picking enclosure patterns over a static dominant-flavor tonic
chicken_pickin.zt_mechanism: Zone-crossing half-step approach to chord tones — same engine as bebop enclosures

pedal_steel_bend.label: Pedal-steel double-stop bends
pedal_steel_bend.description: Emulated pedal-steel bends creating chromatic approach from above
pedal_steel_bend.zt_mechanism: SEMITONE_APPROACH: bII→I zone-crossing, same mechanism as flamenco bII cadence

nashville_turnaround.label: Nashville number turnaround
nashville_turnaround.description: Standard country turnaround — identical to jazz rhythm changes A-section
nashville_turnaround.zt_mechanism: Dominant cycle in 4ths; each V7 resolves via front-door tritone gravity

double_stop_thirds.label: Double-stop 3rds and 6ths
double_stop_thirds.description: Diatonic thirds and sixths across I–IV–V, hybrid or flatpick
double_stop_thirds.zt_mechanism: Guide tones (3rd + 6th) track zone membership across chord changes

travis_picking.label: Travis picking fingerstyle
travis_picking.description: Alternating thumb bass + finger melody, Merle Travis / Chet Atkins style
travis_picking.zt_mechanism: Bass line traces root motion (front-door V→I); melody implies chord tones

country_blues_hybrid.label: Country blues hybrid
country_blues_hybrid.description: Cross between Delta blues and country phrasing — hybrid picking + bends
country_blues_hybrid.zt_mechanism: front_door + semitone approach; same as blues corpus but played clean + twangy

iii_vi_ii_v_country.label: iii–vi–ii–V country modulation
iii_vi_ii_v_country.description: Extended dominant cycle resolution — common in country ballads
iii_vi_ii_v_country.zt_mechanism: Each dominant infers next root via -7 gravity chain; all front-door resolutions

exercises.title: Chicken-pickin' root enclosure
exercises.focus: Snap-mute string skip to root, chromatic lower approach. Brent Mason style.
exercises.zt_note: Lower chromatic enclosure to root = zone-crossing half-step, identical to jazz enclosure_lower_to_3.

exercises.title: Chicken-pickin' 5th enclosure
exercises.focus: Snap to 5th with chromatic approach from below; add open-string pull-off.
exercises.zt_note: 5th is same zone as root (even parity in G: D=2 mod 2=0). Approach is zone-crossing.

exercises.title: Chicken-pickin' 3rd enclosure
exercises.focus: Target major 3rd (B) with double chromatic enclosure, snap dynamic.
exercises.zt_note: B is zone 1 (odd). Approach from C# (zone 1) above and Bb (zone 1) below — both same zone, approach creates brief zone ambiguity before landing.

exercises.title: Pedal-steel double-stop bend (G string)
exercises.focus: Bend G+B strings simultaneously one whole step, emulating E9 pedal steel. Target chord tones of I on release.
exercises.zt_note: Whole-step bend crosses one zone boundary. Landing note is zone 0; departure is zone 1. Pure semitone gravity.

exercises.title: Pedal-steel oblique bend (B string hold)
exercises.focus: Hold high B string, bend G string up one step. Creates pedal-held 5th over moving bass.
exercises.zt_note: Held note (B) is zone 1; bent note moves from zone 0 to zone 1. Resolution creates zone unification = stable landing.

exercises.title: Nashville turnaround in G
exercises.focus: I7–VI7–ii7–V7 two-bar turnaround. Resolve with scalar descent on V7 to I.
exercises.zt_note: Identical dominant cycle to jazz rhythm changes. Each dominant infers next tonic via gravity_chain(root, steps=1).

exercises.title: Nashville turnaround in C
exercises.focus: I7–VI7–ii7–V7 in C. Guide tones: track 3rd and 7th of each dominant.
exercises.zt_note: Tritone axes: C7(E/Bb), A7(C#/G), Dm7 non-dominant, G7(B/F). B/F axis is the gravity resolution into C.

exercises.title: iii–vi–ii–V–I extended country turnaround
exercises.focus: Bm7–E7–Am7–D7–G four-bar ballad resolution. Each guide tone voice-leads by half step.
exercises.zt_note: Five-chord chain. E7 tritone (G#/D) resolves to A; D7 tritone (F#/C) resolves to G. Full ZT gravity chain demonstration.

exercises.title: Diatonic 3rds across I–IV–V in G
exercises.focus: Ascending/descending 3rd intervals across G major. Every double-stop implies its parent chord.
exercises.zt_note: Pairs of notes always straddle zone boundary (minor 3rd) or share zone (major 3rd). A useful ear-training map of zone membership.

exercises.title: Diatonic 6ths in A — Chet Atkins style
exercises.focus: Major/minor 6th pairs ascending through I–IV–V. Fingerpick with thumb-and-finger for Chet tone.
exercises.zt_note: 6th interval = major 3rd inverted. Zone mapping mirrors thirds but reversed. Both notes land on same zone in major 6ths.

exercises.title: Travis picking pattern in C — basic
exercises.focus: Alternating thumb (6-4 strings), melody on 1-2-3. Thumb sets steady pulse; fingers handle the melody.
exercises.zt_note: Static harmony — no zone-crossing gravity. Practice ground for pure rhythmic subdivision.

exercises.title: Travis picking with moving melody in G
exercises.focus: Melody moves over I–IV–V bass pattern. Target chord tones on downbeats; use chromatic passing on upbeats.
exercises.zt_note: Chromatic passing tones on upbeats = zone-crossing motion. Chord tone landings = zone resolution. The ZT engine in a fingerpicking context.

exercises.title: Country blues — 12-bar in G
exercises.focus: G major pentatonic with chromatic passing. Clean amp tone, Telecaster twang. Bends in G minor pentatonic position for blue notes.
exercises.zt_note: Blue notes (b3, b5, b7) are zone-crossing tones played over same-zone chord tones — the tension is literal zone conflict.

exercises.title: Country blues turnaround lick in E
exercises.focus: Classic I7–VI7–ii7–V7 country turnaround with open-string pull-offs. Ends on V ready for verse 2.
exercises.zt_note: bVII7 (D7) is the backdoor dominant to E — same gravity, different entrance. Tests dual-axis gravity in country context.

zt_demonstration.label: Zone-Tritone in country context
zt_demonstration.description: Four exercises that demonstrate ZT gravity in increasingly complex country settings
